page: Amazon Web Services g6e.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 vCPUs, consisting of 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

The instance includes a dedicated NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, offering 45 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the server is provisioned with a 600 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps, facilitating efficient data transfer for distributed workloads.

Performance benchmarks show top-tier capabilities in LLM inference. For prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models, the server ranks in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and general integer operations fall within the average range. Large-block memory write bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%.

Given the inclusion of a high-capacity NVIDIA L40S GPU and 128 GB of RAM, this instance offers a specialized hardware profile tailored for resource-dense workloads. It is highly suited for large language model inference, graphics-intensive rendering, and machine learning workloads that benefit from dedicated GPU acceleration and local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual server designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA L40S GPU providing 45 GB of VRAM, alongside a 600 GB NVMe SSD and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in LLM inference, ranking in the top 10% for prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes. Single-core CPU performance is strong, though multi-core and memory write benchmarks remain average to weak. This hardware configuration is highly suited for AI inference, graphics rendering, and memory-intensive GPU workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 600 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.4xlarge features 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 600 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU for top-tier LLM inference workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring an NVIDIA L40S GPU and local NVMe storage, optimized for high-performance machine learning inference.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU with 16 vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM
NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM for accelerated computing
600 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark performance in LLM inference and prompt processing
Well-suited for machine learning, graphics rendering, and database operations
